subject: refactor(content): improve Content tab UX with 17 fixes
body:
Major UX improvements to the Content tab addressing mobile usability,
visual noise, confusing labels, and code quality issues.

Changes:
- Extract shared ColorPicker and AlignmentPicker components (#16-17)
- Mobile touch targets: larger buttons/swatches on mobile via sm: breakpoints (#7)
- Toolbar: grouped with separators, horizontal scroll on narrow screens (#8)
- Controls: grid layout that stacks properly on mobile (#10)
- MiniCellGrid: full-width on mobile for better touch targets (#14)
- Fix stale markdown hint — now says "Use the toolbar above" (#1)
- Collapse styling rows behind gear toggle button (#2)
- Rename "Auto" alignment to "Default" with dimmed center-align icon (#3)
- Add clear text button (×) on textareas when content exists (#4)
- Add positional hints to cell labels (top, left, etc.) (#5)
- Title & Tagline expanded by default, content preview on collapsed headers (#6)
- Auto-grow textareas based on content lines (#9)
- Rename "Sp:" to "Spacing" with full word labels (#11)
- Scroll textarea into view on mobile keyboard focus (#12)
- Rename "Structured" to "Guided" with mode subtitle (#13)
- Fix mutable cellIndex in AdCanvas renderGridLayout (#15)
- Pre-compute cell mapping in MiniCellGrid via useMemo (#15)

Tags: ux, mobile, content, refactor, accessibility
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: content-tab-ux
Semver: minor
